invariants.description: Every mounted request and repair attempt observes a single MVCC snapshot epoch; repair cannot write back data decoded from an epoch older than the client-visible block generation.
Client writes and repair writeback for overlapping blocks must enter the same serializer before either path may mutate live image state.
Repair writeback must not overwrite a block with dirty client data that has not crossed the fsync/fsyncdir durability boundary.
Only fsync and fsyncdir can publish durability evidence that a repair writeback may rely on; flush remains non-durable.
Mutating repair must hold an active per-image ownership lease before replacing source blocks or refreshing repair symbols.
Repair symbols must match the current block generation before repair writeback can proceed.
Cancellation must leave either no mutation or a fully ledgered cleanup state with no hidden partial repair.
A failed or halfway source-block writeback must not refresh repair symbols, because that would make stale parity look authoritative.

states.description: Mounted scrub can observe corruption and write evidence, but cannot mutate source blocks or symbols.
Corruption evidence is converted into a repair plan and confidence decision before any mutation is attempted.
A mutating repair path owns the per-image repair lease and can perform preflight checks.
Mounted read-write client data or metadata mutation is active or dirty in cache.
All relevant client writes reached an fsync or fsyncdir boundary and are eligible for repair preflight.
Historical bd-rchk0.1.1 state: read-write mounted repair requested mutation before the unified serializer existed, so the path failed closed.
Decoded block writeback is staged behind the same serializer used by client writes.
Future state: repair symbols are refreshed only after source block writeback is verified durable.
Repair writeback failed or was interrupted; symbol refresh is refused and reproduction artifacts are preserved.
Lease, staging rows, and temporary artifacts reached a ledgered cleanup state.

transitions.guard: detection-only evidence may be planned without mutation
mutating repair may proceed to preflight only after exclusive lease acquisition
read-write mounted automatic repair has no unified serializer with client writes
flush cannot advance visible/durable state for repair writeback
fsync/fsyncdir creates durable evidence but still does not authorize RW repair mutation
repair cannot use dirty or merely flushed client data as authoritative
repair symbol generation must match the block generation after client write serialization
mutating repair cannot proceed without active ownership
future path only; current policy keeps the serializer unavailable
source block writeback must be verified durable before symbol refresh
halfway repair failure must preserve repro artifacts and refuse symbol refresh
cancellation releases the lease and preserves ledgered cleanup artifacts

rejection_cases.condition: read-write mounted automatic repair requested before a unified serializer exists
client write overlaps the repaired block range
dirty client data reached flush but not fsync/fsyncdir
repair symbol generation does not match the current source block generation
mutating repair lacks an active per-image ownership lease
source block writeback failed before verification

scenarios.title: Historical missing-serializer gate fails closed while a client write is in flight
Repair plan must name the MVCC snapshot epoch before any mutation decision
Flush cannot authorize repair writeback or symbol refresh
Repair preflight rejects dirty client data that has not crossed fsync or fsyncdir
Mutating repair refuses missing or expired ownership leases
Stale repair symbols are rejected before source block writeback
Cancellation preserves cleanup evidence and does not refresh repair symbols
Halfway writeback failure refuses repair-symbol refresh

operation_dependencies.rationale: decode cannot start until the scrub ledger names the corrupted source block
source-block writeback requires a decoded candidate block
fsync can only publish client data that was already staged
symbols may refresh only after source block writeback is verified
reopen checks observe the durable state left by unmount cleanup
retry evidence must be tied to a prior abort record

options.decision: Historical bd-rchk0.1.1 gate: reject read-write mounted automatic repair mutation until client writes and repair writeback share one serializer.
options.rationale: Operator-visible refusal is inconvenient but does not risk silent user-data overwrite.

options.decision: Enable repair writeback during read-write mounts without a shared serializer.
options.rationale: A repair writeback can overwrite newer client data or refresh symbols from stale source blocks.

options.decision: Current bd-rchk0.1.4 gate: enable only after bd-rchk0.1.2 through bd-rchk0.1.4 produce model, mounted, stale-snapshot, interruption, and writeback-cache-disabled evidence.
options.rationale: A single serializer is admissible after evidence exists, but it carries implementation complexity and performance costs.

follow_up_beads.reason: Refine this contract into executable trace/state tests with shrinking and false-positive guards.
Implement or prove the unified serializer used by client writes and repair writeback.
Prove repair-symbol freshness and stale-generation refusal through the mounted write path.
Prove halfway writeback failure, cleanup, and symbol-refresh refusal under interruption.
